---
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---
```{r, setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(haven)
library(survey)
library(broom)
library(ggplot2)
library(purrr)
library(pROC)
library(car) 
library(survey)
library(surveyROC)
library(svyROC)
library(srvyr)
library(vroom)
library(caret)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file())
data_path_2000_2015 <- file.path("data", "NHTSA_databases", "NHTSA All files")
```


```{r get MADYMO HIC values and build MADYMO dataframe, eval=FALSE}
# create dataframe with MADYMO data
BELTUSE_BIN <- c(0, 1)
BAGDEPLOY_BIN <- c(0, 1)
SEATPOS <- c(11, 13)
madymo_DV_MPH <- 5:65

MADYMO_df <- data.frame()
MADYMO_df <- expand.grid(
  BELTUSE_BIN = BELTUSE_BIN,
  BAGDEPLOY_BIN = BAGDEPLOY_BIN,
  SEATPOS = SEATPOS,
  DV_MPH = madymo_DV_MPH
)

MADYMO_df$BELTUSE_BIN <- as.factor(MADYMO_df$BELTUSE_BIN)
MADYMO_df$BAGDEPLOY_BIN <- as.factor(MADYMO_df$BAGDEPLOY_BIN)
MADYMO_df$SEATPOS <- as.factor(MADYMO_df$SEATPOS)

# write madymo dataframe (without HIC data) to .csv file for reference outside of this code
# write.csv(MADYMO_df, file = file.path("data", "MADYMO_df.csv"))

MADYMO_df <- MADYMO_df %>%
  mutate(
    file_path = file.path(
      "data",
      "minimal_simulations",
      paste0(DV_MPH, "_mph"),
      ifelse(SEATPOS == 11, "driver", "pass"),
      ifelse(BELTUSE_BIN == 0, "nobelt", "belt"),
      ifelse(BAGDEPLOY_BIN == 0, "noairbag", "airbag"),
      "madymo",
      "madymo.peak"
    )
  )

MADYMO_df <- MADYMO_df %>%
  mutate(
    HIC15 = NA,
    T1_15 = NA,
    T2_15 = NA,
    HIC36 = NA,
    T1_36 = NA,
    T2_36 = NA,
    PEAK_LACC = NA,
    PEAK_AACC = NA
  )


# check if all files exist
file_exist_errors <- list()
for (i in MADYMO_df$file_path[1:nrow(MADYMO_df)]) {
  if (!file.exists(file.path(i))) {
    file_exist_errors[i] <- i}
}

# print error message
if (length(file_exist_errors) != 0) {
  cat("Files missing:", paste(file_exist_errors, "\n"))
} else {
  cat("No MADYMO files missing")
}

# Initialize a list to store file names that caused errors
error_names <- c()

# Loop through each file in the "file_path_name" column of MADYMO_df
# Loop through each file in the "file_path" column of MADYMO_df
for (i in 1:nrow(MADYMO_df)) {  # Process the first 5 files for testing
  file_name <- MADYMO_df$file_path[i]
  cat("Processing file:", file_name, "\n")
  
  # Use tryCatch to handle errors (like missing files)
  result <- tryCatch({
    # Step 1: Read the file into R
    cat("Attempting to read file:", file_name, "\n")
    lines <- readLines(file_name)
    
    print(paste("Loading", file_name))
    
    # Initialize empty variables to store the extracted values
    HIC15 <- NA
    T1_15 <- NA
    T2_15 <- NA
    HIC36 <- NA
    T1_36 <- NA
    T2_36 <- NA
    PEAK_LACC <- NA
    PEAK_AACC <- NA
    
    cat("Successfully read file:", file_name, "\n")
    
    # Function to extract the numeric values from the line
    extract_hic_values <- function(line) {
      # Extract VALUE
      value <- as.numeric(gsub(".*VALUE[^0-9]*([0-9\\.]+).*", "\\1", line))
      
      # Extract T1
      t1 <- as.numeric(gsub(".*T1\\s*=\\s*([0-9\\.]+).*", "\\1", line))
      
      # Extract T2
      t2 <- as.numeric(gsub(".*T2\\s*=\\s*([0-9\\.]+).*", "\\1", line))
      
      return(list(value = value, t1 = t1, t2 = t2))
    }
    
    # Function to extract the third number (maximum resultant acceleration) from the "Res. acceleration" line
    extract_res_acceleration <- function(line) {
      # Use regular expression to capture all numbers, including scientific notation
      matches <- regmatches(line, gregexpr("[+-]?\\d*\\.?\\d+(E[+-]?\\d+)?", line))
      
      # Extract the third number from the matched results
      if (length(matches[[1]]) >= 3) {
        res_acc <- as.numeric(matches[[1]][4])  # The third number
      } else {
        res_acc <- NA
      }
      
      return(res_acc)
    }
    
    # Step 2: Loop through the lines to find relevant information
    for (j in 1:(length(lines) - 2)) {
      
      # Check for HIC15_inj line
      if (grepl("HIC15_inj", lines[j])) {
        # Extract VALUE, T1, and T2 from the next-next line
        value_line <- lines[j + 2]
        
        values_15 <- extract_hic_values(value_line)
        HIC15 <- values_15$value
        T1_15 <- values_15$t1
        T2_15 <- values_15$t2
      }
      
      # Check for HIC36_inj line
      if (grepl("HIC36_inj", lines[j])) {
        # Extract VALUE, T1, and T2 from the next-next line
        value_line <- lines[j + 2]
        
        values_36 <- extract_hic_values(value_line)
        HIC36 <- values_36$value
        T1_36 <- values_36$t1
        T2_36 <- values_36$t2
      }
      
      # Check for the /HumanMale50%/HeadCG_acc identifier line
      if (grepl("/HumanMale50%/HeadCG_acc", lines[j])) {
        cat("Found /HumanMale50%/HeadCG_acc at line", j, "\n")
        
        # Look for the "Res. acceleration" line in the next few lines
        for (k in (j+1):(j+5)) {  # We check the next 5 lines to find the "Res. acceleration" line
          if (grepl("Res. acceleration", lines[k])) {
            cat("Found Res. acceleration at line", k, "\n")
            
            # Extract the maximum resultant acceleration
            PEAK_LACC <- extract_res_acceleration(lines[k])
            cat("Extracted max resultant acceleration (m/s/s) (PEAK_LACC):", PEAK_LACC, "\n")
            break
          }
        }
      }
      
      # Check for the /HumanMale50%/Head_aac identifier line
      if (grepl("/HumanMale50%/Head_aac", lines[j])) {
        cat("Found /HumanMale50%/Head_aac at line", j, "\n")
        
        # Look for the "Res. ang. acc." line in the next few lines
        for (k in (j+1):(j+5)) {  # We check the next 5 lines to find the "Res. ang. acc." line
          if (grepl("Res. ang. acc.", lines[k])) {
            cat("Found Res. ang. acc. at line", k, "\n")
            
            # Extract the maximum resultant acceleration
            PEAK_AACC <- extract_res_acceleration(lines[k])
            cat("Extracted max resultant acceleration (PEAK_AACC):", PEAK_AACC, "\n")
            break
          }
        }
      }
    }
    
    # Step 3: Assign the extracted values to the corresponding row in MADYMO_df
    MADYMO_df$HIC15[i] <- HIC15
    MADYMO_df$T1_15[i] <- T1_15
    MADYMO_df$T2_15[i] <- T2_15
    MADYMO_df$HIC36[i] <- HIC36
    MADYMO_df$T1_36[i] <- T1_36
    MADYMO_df$T2_36[i] <- T2_36
    MADYMO_df$PEAK_LACC[i] <- PEAK_LACC
    MADYMO_df$PEAK_AACC[i] <- PEAK_AACC
    
    TRUE  # Indicate success
  }, error = function(e) {
    # Add the file name to error_names if there was an error
    cat("Error in processing file:", file_name, "\n")
    error_names <<- c(error_names, file_name)
    FALSE  # Indicate failure
  })
  
  # Log successful or failed processing
  if (result) {
    cat("File processed successfully:", file_name, "\n")
  } else {
    cat("Skipping file due to error:", file_name, "\n")
  }
}

# convert LACC to g
MADYMO_df$PEAK_LACC_g <- MADYMO_df$PEAK_LACC / 9.81

# Print the list of files that caused errors
if (length(error_names) > 0) {
  cat(length(error_names), " Files with errors:", paste(error_names, collapse = ", "), "\n")
} else {
  cat("No errors occurred during processing.\n")
}

MADYMO_df$SEATPOS <- as.factor(MADYMO_df$SEATPOS)
MADYMO_df$BELTUSE_BIN <- as.factor(MADYMO_df$BELTUSE_BIN)
MADYMO_df$BAGDEPLOY_BIN <- as.factor(MADYMO_df$BAGDEPLOY_BIN)

# vroom write MADYMO_df to csv
vroom::vroom_write(MADYMO_df, file = file.path("data", "MADYMO_df.csv"))


```


```{r create nhtsa dataframe, eval=FALSE}
# Helper function to process data for each year
vehicle_count <- 0
individual_count <- 0

process_year <- function(year) {
  # Load datasets (replace paths as necessary)
  # occupant injury
  oi <- read_sas(file.path(data_path_2000_2015, paste0(year, "oi.sas7bdat")))
  # occupant 
  oa <- read_sas(file.path(data_path_2000_2015, paste0(year, "oa.sas7bdat")))
  # general vehicle
  gv <- read_sas(file.path(data_path_2000_2015, paste0(year, "gv.sas7bdat")))
  # vehicle event
  ve <- read_sas(file.path(data_path_2000_2015, paste0(year, "ve.sas7bdat")))
  # accident
  accident <- read_sas(file.path(data_path_2000_2015, paste0(year, "accident.sas7bdat")))
  
  print(paste("Year:", year))
  
  
  # Injury processing
  injury_temp <- oi %>%
    mutate(
      # Handle column name inconsistencies
      BODYREG = if ("BODYREG" %in% names(.)) BODYREG else bodyreg,
      LESION = if ("LESION" %in% names(.)) LESION else lesion
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      BACK = ifelse(BODYREG == "B", 1, 0),
      NECK = ifelse(BODYREG == "N", 1, 0),
      CONCUSSION = ifelse(LESION == "K", 1, 0),
      AIS1 = ifelse(AIS >= 1 & AIS <= 6, 1, 0)
    )
  
  
  print(paste("all injuries count:", nrow(injury_temp)))
  ifelse(year == 2000, print(colnames(injury_temp)), print(""))
  
  # Summarize injuries (1 row per occupant)
  injury_summary <- injury_temp %>%
    group_by(PSU, CASENO, VEHNO, OCCNO, RATWGT) %>%
    summarise(
      BACK = max(BACK, na.rm = TRUE),
      NECK = max(NECK, na.rm = TRUE),
      CONCUSSION = max(CONCUSSION, na.rm = TRUE),
      AIS1 = max(AIS1, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    # Replace -Inf with NA (from max(na.rm=TRUE when all values are NA)
    mutate(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(is.infinite(.), NA, .)))
  
  print(
    paste(
    "all injured individuals count:", 
    nrow(
      injury_summary)))

  
  # Start with occupant data (oa) and LEFT JOIN injuries
  
  occ <- oa %>%
    # Merge injury data (retain all occupants, even if uninjured)
    left_join(injury_summary, by = c("PSU", "CASENO", "VEHNO", "OCCNO", "RATWGT")) %>%
    # Replace NA with 0 for injury columns (uninjured occupants)
    mutate(
      across(c(BACK, NECK, CONCUSSION, AIS1), ~ replace_na(., 0)),
      OI = ifelse(BACK == 1 | NECK == 1 | CONCUSSION == 1 | AIS1 == 1, 1, 0),
      BELTUSE_BIN = ifelse(MANUSE %in% c(0, 1), 0, ifelse(MANUSE == 4, 1, NA)),
      BAGDEPLOY_BIN = ifelse(BAGDEPLY == 1, 1, 0),
      BMI = WEIGHT / ((HEIGHT / 100) ^ 2),
      BMI_HIGH = ifelse(BMI >= 30, 1, 0)
    ) %>%
    filter(SEATPOS %in% c(11, 13))  # Front-left and front-right seats
    ifelse(year == 2000, print(colnames(occ)), print(""))

    
  # count all individuals involved in all crashes in all years
  oa_grouped <- oa %>%
    group_by(PSU, CASENO, OCCNO, RATWGT)
  individual_count <<- individual_count + nrow(oa_grouped)
  
  
  
  
  print(paste("injured + uninjured count:", nrow(occ)))
  
  
  
  # Vehicle data
  veh <- gv %>%
    inner_join(ve, by = c("PSU", "CASENO", "VEHNO", "RATWGT")) %>%
    mutate(
      # KEEP = ifelse(
      #   DOF1 %in% c(
      #     1, 11, 12, 21, 31, 
      #     32, 41, 51, 52, 61, 
      #     71, 72, 81, 91, 92
      #   ), 1, 0),
      DV_MPH = round(DVTOTAL * 0.621371)
    ) 
  # %>%
  #   filter(KEEP == 1)
  


  # count all vehicles involved in all crashes in all years
  vehicle_count <<- vehicle_count + nrow(gv)
  ifelse(year == 2000, print(colnames(veh)), print(""))

  
  print(paste("gv + veh count:", nrow(veh)))
  
  # front temp
  front_temp <- occ %>%
    inner_join(veh, by = c("PSU", "CASENO", "VEHNO", "RATWGT"))
  
  print(paste("veh + occ count:", nrow(front_temp)))
  
  # front final
  front <- front_temp %>%
    inner_join(accident, by = c("PSU", "CASENO", "RATWGT")) %>%
    filter(EVENTS == 1)
      ifelse(year == 2000, print(colnames(front)), print(""))

      
  print(paste("accident + front count:", nrow(front)))
  
  return(front)
}

# Loop through years and process data
years <- 2000:2015
# years <- 2000
all_data <- lapply(years, process_year)
nhtsa_combined_df <- bind_rows(all_data)

print(paste("all individuals all years before DOF1 filter:", nrow(nhtsa_combined_df)))


# filter out DOF1 %in% c(1, 11, 12, 21, 31, 32, 41, 51, 52, 61, 71, 72, 81, 91, 92)
nhtsa_combined_df <- nhtsa_combined_df %>%
  filter(DOF1 %in% c(1, 11, 12, 21, 31, 32, 41, 51, 52, 61, 71, 72, 81, 91, 92))

print(paste("all individuals all years after DOF1 filter:", nrow(nhtsa_combined_df)))

# write nhtsa_combined_df to csv with vroom
vroom::vroom_write(nhtsa_combined_df, file = file.path("data", "nhtsa_combined_df_saved.csv"))

```


```{r clean and filter nhtsa dataframe}
# read nhtsa_combined_df.csv with vroom
nhtsa_combined_df <- vroom::vroom(file.path("data", "nhtsa_combined_df_saved.csv"))

# Final adjustments
nhtsa_combined_df <- nhtsa_combined_df %>%
  mutate(
    BACK = ifelse(BACK < 1, 0, BACK),
    NECK = ifelse(NECK < 1, 0, NECK),
    CONCUSSION = ifelse(CONCUSSION < 1, 0, CONCUSSION),
    AIS1 = ifelse(AIS1 < 1, 0, AIS1),
    OI = ifelse(OI < 1, 0, OI),
    # need to make sure this is catching the right group
    INJURED = ifelse(OI == 1 | MAIS > 0 | INJNUM > 0, 1, 0),
    SEX_BIN = ifelse(SEX == 1, 0, ifelse(SEX == 9, NA, 1))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    DV_MPH >= 5 & DV_MPH <= 65,
    !is.na(BELTUSE_BIN),
    !is.na(BAGDEPLOY_BIN),
    !is.na(CONCUSSION)
  )

print(paste("all entries all years (before filter):", nrow(nhtsa_combined_df)))

# add categorical MAIS variable
nhtsa_combined_df <- nhtsa_combined_df %>%
  mutate(
    MAIS_cat = as.factor(MAIS),
    DV_MPH_BIN = as.factor(ifelse(DV_MPH <20, 0, 1))
  )

# add HIPR var from CONCUSSION for nhtsa project
nhtsa_combined_df <- nhtsa_combined_df %>%
  mutate(
    HIPR = CONCUSSION
  )

nhtsa_combined_df <- nhtsa_combined_df %>% 
  select(order(colnames(nhtsa_combined_df)))

nhtsa_combined_df <- nhtsa_combined_df %>%
  mutate(
    ID = row_number()
  )

# make appropriate variables into factors
cols_all <- sort(colnames(nhtsa_combined_df))
cols_factor <- c("BAGDEPLY", "BAGDEPLOY_BIN", "BELTUSE_BIN", "BMI_HIGH", "CONCUSSION", "DVEST", "HIPR", "ID", "INJURED", "MAIS", "MAIS_cat", "MANUSE", "OBJCONT1", "OBJCONT2", "OCCNO", "OI", "PSU", "PSUSTRAT", "ROLLOVER", "SEATPOS", "SEX_BIN", "VEHNO", "VEHTYPE", "YEAR", "ALCINV", "DEATH", "DRUGS", "MAKE", "WEATHER")
nhtsa_combined_df[cols_factor] <- lapply(nhtsa_combined_df[cols_factor], factor)

cols_explore <- c(cols_factor, "AGE", "BMI", "DV_MPH", "DV_MPH", "HEIGHT",  "WEIGHT")

explore_df <- subset(nhtsa_combined_df, select = cols_explore)

# add IND_ID var which is a unique identifier for each individual

sort(colnames(nhtsa_combined_df))


# explore data
summary(explore_df)
```


```{r keep only relevant columns}
# columns that will be kept in final dataset
cols_final <- c("ID", "HIPR", "BAGDEPLOY_BIN", "BELTUSE_BIN", "BMI", "DVEST", "AIS1", "MAIS", "OCCNO", "SEATPOS", "SEX_BIN", "YEAR", "AGE", "DV_MPH", "DV_MPH_BIN", "HEIGHT", "WEIGHT", "PSU", "PSUSTRAT", "RATWGT", "BODYTYPE", "ALCINV", "DEATH", "DRUGS", "MAKE", "MODELYR", "VEHWGT", "WEATHER", "TIME", "VEHTYPE")

# select only relevant columns
nhtsa_combined_df <- nhtsa_combined_df %>%
  select(
    all_of(cols_final))

print(paste("all years (after filter):", nrow(nhtsa_combined_df)))

```


```{r join MADYMO data and add transform vars}
# exclude NA values for some variables
# nhtsa_combined_df <- nhtsa_combined_df %>%
#   subset(
#     
#   )

# read MADYMO_df.csv with vroom
MADYMO_df <- vroom::vroom(file.path("data", "MADYMO_df.csv"))

# make all relevant variables correct datatypes
MADYMO_df$SEATPOS <- as.factor(MADYMO_df$SEATPOS)
MADYMO_df$BELTUSE_BIN <- as.factor(MADYMO_df$BELTUSE_BIN)
MADYMO_df$BAGDEPLOY_BIN <- as.factor(MADYMO_df$BAGDEPLOY_BIN)

# join MADYMO data to NHTSA dataframe
madymo_nhtsa_df <- nhtsa_combined_df %>%
  left_join(MADYMO_df, by = join_by(DV_MPH == DVTOTAL, SEATPOS == SEATPOS, BELTUSE_BIN == BELTUSE_BIN, BAGDEPLOY_BIN == BAGDEPLOY_BIN))

# transform/scale HIC15 to see if numbers are more legible
madymo_nhtsa_df$HIC15_thousand <-
  madymo_nhtsa_df$HIC15/1000

# create log transformed HIC15
madymo_nhtsa_df$HIC15_log <- 
  log(madymo_nhtsa_df$HIC15)

# create sqrt transformed HIC15
madymo_nhtsa_df$HIC15_sqrt <- 
  sqrt(madymo_nhtsa_df$HIC15)
```

```{r print summary of relevant variables}
# print summary of relevant variables
summary(madymo_nhtsa_df)
```

```{r extreme variables}
# preserve madymo_nhtsa_df before removing extreme values
madymo_nhtsa_df_full <- madymo_nhtsa_df
# examine extreme values
extreme_values <- madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
  select_if(is.numeric) %>%
  gather(variable, value) %>%
  group_by(variable) %>%
  summarise(
    min = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    max = max(value, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(min)), desc(abs(max)))

# examine values
print(extreme_values, n = 23)

# create list of IDs with omittable values
extreme_value_ids <- c()

# MODELYR values all reasonable
# all MADYMO measurements come from simulation, not subject to incorrect input
# YEAR was inches before 1993, but this is not included in the dataset
# WEIGHT and HEIGHT were imperial (pounds and inches) before 1993, but that is not relevant for this dataset
# WEIGHT has maximum 150, all values above 150 are combined
# HEIGHT has maximum 220, all values over 200 are combined

# investigate BMI (instead of HEIGHT and WEIGHT separately - helps identify unusual values)
bmi_df <- madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
  filter(BMI < 10 | BMI > 70) %>%
  select(ID, YEAR, BMI, HEIGHT, WEIGHT, AGE)
# ID 858 has BMI 1.73 (height 170 cm, weight 5 kg, age 39) - probably a data entry error; can omit
extreme_value_ids <- c(
  extreme_value_ids,
  858
)
# ID 1902 and 9191 are 5.7 and 7.4 BMI, (heights 157, 168 and weights 14, 21 and ages 34, 47), unsure about these
extreme_value_ids <- c(
  extreme_value_ids,
  1902, 9191
)
# except ID 7553, all HEIGHTS with BMI > 100 appear to be input as inches (between 40 and 75). consider re-coding these
# re-code HEIGHT of IDs where BMI > 101 and HEIGHT < 75, from inches to cm
extreme_values_df <- madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
  filter(BMI > 101 & HEIGHT < 75) %>%
  mutate(HEIGHT = HEIGHT * 2.54) %>%
  select(ID, HEIGHT)
# copy new recoded values back to madymo_nhtsa_df
madymo_nhtsa_df <- madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
  rows_update(extreme_values_df, by = "ID") %>%
  # recalculate BMI with new HEIGHT values
  mutate(BMI = WEIGHT / ((HEIGHT / 100) ^ 2))
  
# TIME values all expected, within 0001 and 2400

# investigate VEHWGT
# histogram of VEHWGT
ggplot(madymo_nhtsa_df, aes(x = VEHWGT)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10) +
  labs(title = "Histogram of VEHWGT", x = "VEHWGT", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
# examine extreme values
vehwgt_df <- madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
  filter(VEHWGT < 1000 | VEHWGT > 5000) %>%
  select(ID, VEHWGT, BODYTYPE, HEIGHT)
# no non-zero weights below 1400; no non-9999 weights over 5177
# count of 9999 or 0 values:
vehwgt_extreme_df <- madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
  filter(VEHWGT %in% c(9999, 0)) %>%
  select(ID, VEHWGT, BODYTYPE, HEIGHT) 
vehwgt_extreme_count <- nrow(vehwgt_extreme_df)
# count: 194
vehwgt_extreme_count / nrow(madymo_nhtsa_df) * 100
# 194 is 1.499% of dataset, therefore can be omitted
# add IDs to extreme_value_ids
extreme_value_ids <- c(
  extreme_value_ids,
  vehwgt_extreme_df$ID
)

# investigate AGE
# histogram of AGE
ggplot(madymo_nhtsa_df, aes(x = AGE)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Histogram of AGE", x = "AGE", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
# all values within 0 and 97 (max)

# exclude vars with normal ranges (can adjust which groups are added to the list; VEHWGT for example can be excluded)
madymo_nhtsa_df <- madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
  filter(!ID %in% extreme_value_ids)
```

```{r functions for survey model evaluation}
# function to print and examine ROC curve, AUC, and other metrics
roc_weighted_fun <- function(design, model) {
  # Extract required components from survey design
  roc_data <- data.frame(
    y = design$variables$HIPR,
    phat = fitted(model),
    weights = design$variables$RATWGT
  )

  
  # Create weighted ROC object
  mycurve <- wroc(
    response.var = "y",
    phat.var = "phat",
    weights.var = "weights",
    data = roc_data,
    tag.event = levels(roc_data$y)[2],
    tag.nonevent = levels(roc_data$y)[1],
    cutoff.method = "Youden"
  )
  
  # Calculate prevalence of the event
  event_level <- levels(roc_data$y)[2]
  prevalence <- sum(roc_data$weights[roc_data$y == event_level]) / sum(roc_data$weights)
    
  # browser()
  
  # Extract performance metrics
  metrics_list <- list(
    AUC = mycurve$wauc,
    Optimal_Cutoff = mycurve$optimal.cutoff$cutoff.value,
    Sensitivity = mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Sew,
    Specificity = mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Spw,
    # PPV
    PPV = 100 * ((mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Sew * prevalence) / 
                   ((mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Sew * prevalence) + 
                      ((1 - mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Spw) * (1 - prevalence))))
  )
  
  # Generate plot
  roc_plot <- wroc.plot(
    x = mycurve,
    print.auc = TRUE,
    print.cutoff = TRUE
  )
  
  # Return both plot and metrics
  return(list(
    mycurve = mycurve,
    metrics = metrics_list
  ))
}


# function for full model performance evaluation
model_eval <- function(design, model) {
  roc_weighted_fun(
    design = design,
    model = model
  )
}
```


```{r survey model definition}
# build survey design object before adjust extreme values
survey_design_before_extreme_adjustment <- svydesign(
  id = ~PSU,
  strata = ~PSUSTRAT,
  weights = ~RATWGT,
  data = madymo_nhtsa_df_full
)

# build survey design object after adjust extreme values
survey_design <- svydesign(
  id = ~PSU,
  strata = ~PSUSTRAT,
  weights = ~RATWGT,
  data = madymo_nhtsa_df
)

# survey logistic model before adjusting extreme values
survey_model_full <- svyglm(
  HIPR ~ HIC15_log,
  design = survey_design_before_extreme_adjustment,
  family = quasibinomial
 )
summary(survey_model_full)

# survey logistic model after adjusting extreme values
survey_model_extreme_adjusted <- svyglm(
  HIPR ~ HIC15_log,
  design = survey_design,
  family = quasibinomial
 )
summary(survey_model_extreme_adjusted)


# use model eval function on both models
model_eval_full <- model_eval(
  survey_design_before_extreme_adjustment, 
  survey_model_full
)

model_eval_extreme_adjusted <- model_eval(
  survey_design, 
  survey_model_extreme_adjusted
)

```

```{r examine missingness and extreme values}
# examine missingness
missing_values <- madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
  summarise_all(~ sum(is.na(.))) %>%
  gather(variable, missing) %>%
  filter(missing > 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(missing))

print(missing_values)


# TO DO: find missing randomness, make decisions for handling vars based on this
```

```{r baseline descriptives}
# Create cases and controls data frames
cases_df <- madymo_nhtsa_df %>% filter(HIPR == 1)
controls_df <- madymo_nhtsa_df %>% filter(HIPR == 0)

# Define variables with labels and types
baseline_vars <- list(
  list(var_name = "AGE", label = "Age", type = "continuous"),
  list(var_name = "BMI", label = "BMI in kg/m²", type = "continuous"),
  list(var_name = "SEX_BIN", label = "Sex (%)", type = "categorical"),
  list(var_name = "SEATPOS", label = "Seat Position", type = "categorical"),
  list(var_name = "BELTUSE_BIN", label = "Belt Use (%)", type = "categorical"),
  list(var_name = "BAGDEPLOY_BIN", label = "Airbag Deployment (%)", type = "categorical")
)

# Initialize empty dataframe for results
baseline_descriptives <- data.frame(
  Characteristic = character(),
  Value = character(),
  Cohort = character(),
  With_head_injury = character(),
  P_Value = character(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Function to format p-values
format_p <- function(p) {
  ifelse(p < 0.001, "<0.001", round(p, digits = 3))
}

# Process each variable
for (var in baseline_vars) {
  if (var$type == "continuous") {
    # Continuous variables
    case_mean <- mean(cases_df[[var$var_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
    case_sd <- sd(cases_df[[var$var_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
    control_mean <- mean(controls_df[[var$var_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
    control_sd <- sd(controls_df[[var$var_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
    
    p_value <- t.test(cases_df[[var$var_name]], controls_df[[var$var_name]])$p.value
    
    baseline_descriptives <- rbind(
      baseline_descriptives,
      data.frame(
        Characteristic = var$label,
        Value = "Mean (SD)",
        Cohort = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f)", control_mean, control_sd),
        With_head_injury = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f)", case_mean, case_sd),
        P_Value = format_p(p_value),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    )
  } else {
    # Categorical variables
    # Get counts and percentages
    case_counts <- table(cases_df[[var$var_name]])
    case_pct <- prop.table(case_counts) * 100
    control_counts <- table(controls_df[[var$var_name]])
    control_pct <- prop.table(control_counts) * 100
    
    # Statistical test
    cont_table <- table(madymo_nhtsa_df[[var$var_name]], madymo_nhtsa_df$HIPR)
    test_result <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(cont_table))
    if (any(test_result$expected < 5)) test_result <- fisher.test(cont_table)
    
    # Add header row
    baseline_descriptives <- rbind(
      baseline_descriptives,
      data.frame(
        Characteristic = var$label,
        Value = "",
        Cohort = "",
        With_head_injury = "",
        P_Value = format_p(test_result$p.value),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    )
    
    # Add levels
    for (level in levels(madymo_nhtsa_df[[var$var_name]])) {
      baseline_descriptives <- rbind(
        baseline_descriptives,
        data.frame(
          Characteristic = "",
          Value = level,
          Cohort = sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", control_counts[level], control_pct[level]),
          With_head_injury = sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", case_counts[level], case_pct[level]),
          P_Value = "",
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      )
    }
  }
}

# View the final table
print(baseline_descriptives)


# rethink what groups are split by in baseline descriptives (outcome might not be best way to do it, but no predetermined stratification variable like sex, like seen in other papers)
# not difficult to do, so can just adjust code after meeting with maurice

```


```{r define multivariate groups}
# define multivariate variable groups
occ_vars <- c("SEX", "AGE", "BMI")
env_vars <- c("WEATHER", "MAKE", "MODELYR", "VEHWGT")
madymo_vars <- c("DV_MPH", "SEATPOS", "BELTUSE_BIN", "BAGDEPLOY_BIN")
```



```{r univariate logistic regression}
# univariate logistic regression
# examine correlation between DV_MPH and HIC15
```



```{r comparing model performance}
compared_models <- c(
  "model_eval_full", 
  "model_eval_extreme_adjusted")

# compare models in dataframe using loop
comparison_df <- data.frame()
for (model in compared_models) {
  # Extract the AUC and other metrics
  auc <- get(model)$metrics$AUC
  optimal_cutoff <- get(model)$metrics$Optimal_Cutoff
  sensitivity <- get(model)$metrics$Sensitivity
  specificity <- get(model)$metrics$Specificity
  PPV <- get(model)$metrics$PPV
  
  # Create a new row for the comparison dataframe
  new_row <- data.frame(
    Model = model,
    AUC = auc,
    Optimal_Cutoff = optimal_cutoff,
    Sensitivity = sensitivity,
    Specificity = specificity,
    PPV = PPV
  )
  
  # Append the new row to the comparison dataframe
  comparison_df <- rbind(comparison_df, new_row)
}
```


```{r non-survey logistic regression normal, eval=FALSE}
# vif(lm(DV_MPH ~ HIC15_thousand, data = madymo_nhtsa_df))

model <- glm(HIPR ~ HIC15_thousand, 
            data = madymo_nhtsa_df, family = "binomial")

# roc for model
roc_obj_crude <- roc(madymo_nhtsa_df$HIPR, predict(model, type = "response"))
plot(roc_obj_crude, main = "ROC Curve for Crude Model")

summary(glm(HIPR ~ HIC15_thousand + 
              DV_MPH, 
            data = madymo_nhtsa_df, family = "binomial"))

summary(glm(HIPR ~ HIC15_thousand*DV_MPH + 
              DV_MPH + 
              BELTUSE_BIN, 
            data = madymo_nhtsa_df, family = "binomial"))

summary(glm(HIPR ~ HIC15_thousand + 
              DV_MPH + 
              BELTUSE_BIN + 
              SEATPOS + 
              BAGDEPLOY_BIN, 
            data = madymo_nhtsa_df, family = "binomial"))

summary(glm(HIPR ~ HIC15_thousand + 
              DV_MPH + 
              BELTUSE_BIN + 
              SEATPOS + 
              BAGDEPLOY_BIN, 
            data = madymo_nhtsa_df, family = "binomial"))

model <- glm(HIPR ~ HIC15_thousand + 
              DV_MPH + 
              BELTUSE_BIN + 
              SEATPOS + 
              BAGDEPLOY_BIN + 
              AGE + 
              BMI + 
              SEX_BIN, 
            data = madymo_nhtsa_df, family = "binomial", na.action = na.exclude)
summary(model)
# roc for model
roc_obj_final <- roc(madymo_nhtsa_df$HIPR, predict(model, type = "response"))
plot(roc_obj_final, main = "ROC Curve for Final Model")

# get ppv and npv of model
# confusionMatrix(table(predict(model, type = "response") > 0.5, madymo_nhtsa_df$HIPR))

```

```{r non-survey logistic regression log transformed, eval=FALSE}
# visualize 


# # VIF DV_MPH and HIC15_log
# vif(lm(DV_MPH ~ HIC15_log, data = madymo_nhtsa_df))

summary(glm(HIPR ~ HIC15_log, 
            data = madymo_nhtsa_df, family = "binomial"))

summary(glm(HIPR ~ HIC15_log + 
              DV_MPH, 
            data = madymo_nhtsa_df, family = "binomial"))

summary(glm(HIPR ~ HIC15_log + 
              DV_MPH + 
              BELTUSE_BIN, 
            data = madymo_nhtsa_df, family = "binomial"))

summary(glm(HIPR ~ HIC15_log + 
              DV_MPH + 
              BELTUSE_BIN + 
              SEATPOS + 
              BAGDEPLOY_BIN, 
            data = madymo_nhtsa_df, family = "binomial"))

summary(glm(HIPR ~ HIC15_log + 
              DV_MPH + 
              BELTUSE_BIN + 
              SEATPOS + 
              BAGDEPLOY_BIN +
              AGE, 
            data = madymo_nhtsa_df, family = "binomial"))

model <- glm(HIPR ~ HIC15_log + 
              DV_MPH + 
              BELTUSE_BIN + 
              SEATPOS + 
              BAGDEPLOY_BIN + 
              AGE + 
              BMI + 
              SEX_BIN, 
            data = madymo_nhtsa_df, family = "binomial")
summary(model)

# examine correlation between DV_MPH and HIC15_log
cor(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH, madymo_nhtsa_df$HIC15_log)

# get ROC for model
# roc_obj <- roc(madymo_nhtsa_df$HIPR, predict(model, type = "response"))
# fails because different lengths

# get ppv and npv of model
# confusionMatrix(table(predict(model, type = "response") > 0.5, madymo_nhtsa_df$HIPR))

```

```{r make DV_MPH categories, eval=FALSE}
# create DV_MPH_two var where DVMPH is 0 if DVMPH < 35 and 1 if DVMPH >= 35
madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_two <- ifelse(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH < 35, 0, 1)
madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_two <- as.factor(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_two)


# create DV_MPH_three var where DVMPH is 0 if DVMPH < 25, 1 if DV_MPH is 25-45, and 2 if DV_MPH > 45
madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_three <- ifelse(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH < 25, 0, ifelse(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH < 45, 1, 2))
madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_three <- as.factor(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_three)


# create DV_MPH_high var where DVMPH is 0 if DVMPH < 50 and 1 if DVMPH >= 50
madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_high <- ifelse(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH < 50, 0, 1)
madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_high <- as.factor(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_high)
```

```{r survey logistic model, eval=FALSE}
# survey logistic model
# create survey design object with RATWGT as weights, PSU as cluster, and PSUSTRAT as strata
survey_design <- svydesign(
  id = ~PSU,
  strata = ~PSUSTRAT,
  weights = ~RATWGT,
  data = madymo_nhtsa_df
)

# survey logistic model - commented vars ruin model, might be NA-related. need to find a fix
survey_model <- svyglm(
  HIPR ~ HIC15_log + 
    DV_MPH + 
    BELTUSE_BIN + 
    SEATPOS + 
    BAGDEPLOY_BIN + 
    AGE + 
    BMI +
    # BODYTYPE +
    # ALCINV +
    # DEATH +
    # DRUGS +
    # MAKE +
    MODELYR +
    VEHWGT +
    WEATHER,
  design = survey_design,
  family = quasibinomial
 )
summary(survey_model)

#take out least significant vars in order
survey_model <- svyglm(
  HIPR ~ HIC15_log + 
    DV_MPH + 
    BELTUSE_BIN,
  design = survey_design,
  family = quasibinomial
 )
summary(survey_model)



survey_model <- svyglm(
  HIPR ~ HIC15_log + 
    DV_MPH_two + 
    BELTUSE_BIN +
    DV_MPH_two*HIC15_log,
  design = survey_design,
  family = quasibinomial
 )
summary(survey_model)


survey_model <- svyglm(
  HIPR ~ HIC15_log + 
    DV_MPH_three + 
    BELTUSE_BIN +
    DV_MPH_three*HIC15_log,
  design = survey_design,
  family = quasibinomial
 )
summary(survey_model)




survey_model <- svyglm(
  HIPR ~ HIC15_log + 
    DV_MPH_high + 
    BELTUSE_BIN +
    DV_MPH_high*HIC15_log,
  design = survey_design,
  family = quasibinomial
 )
summary(survey_model)

# positive predictive value for survey_model (does not work yet)
# confusionMatrix(table(predict(survey_model, type = "response") > 0.5, madymo_nhtsa_df$HIPR))
```

```{r visualize outcome variables}
# examine frequency of DV_MPH values for all crashes with histogram
ggplot(madymo_nhtsa_df, aes(x = DV_MPH)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2.5) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Crash Speeds (All)", x = "Crash Speed (mph)", y = "Frequency") +
  # x axis every 5 mph
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 65, by = 5))

# examine frequency of head injury values by speed for all crashes with histogram
ggplot(madymo_nhtsa_df, aes(x = DV_MPH, fill = HIPR)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Crash Speeds by Injury Status (All)", x = "Crash Speed (mph)", y = "Frequency") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "blue", "1" = "red")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 65, by = 5))

# examine frequency of head injury values by speed for all crashes, separated by SEATPOS with histogram
ggplot(madymo_nhtsa_df, aes(x = DV_MPH, fill = HIPR)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Crash Speeds by Injury Status (All)", x = "Crash Speed (mph)", y = "Frequency") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "blue", "1" = "red")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 65, by = 5)) +
  facet_wrap(~SEATPOS) +
  # replace fill names with "Driver" and "Passenger"
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Head injury"))

ggplot(madymo_nhtsa_df, aes(x = DV_MPH, fill = MAIS_cat)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Crash Speeds by Injury Status (All)", x = "Crash Speed (mph)", y = "Frequency") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "blue", "1" = "red")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 65, by = 5)) +
  facet_wrap(~SEATPOS) +
  # replace fill names with "Driver" and "Passenger"
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Head injury"))
```

```{r visualize HIC15 and DV_MPH relationship}

# scatterplot of HIC15_thousand (10,000 scale) and DV_MPH to see relationship
ggplot(madymo_nhtsa_df, aes(y = HIC15, x = DV_MPH)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Scatterplot of HIC15 and DV_MPH", y = "HIC15 (transformed)", x = "DV_MPH") + 
  # y axis to 65
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 60000))


# scatterplot of HIC15_sqrt and DV_MPH because above relationship was nonlinear
ggplot(madymo_nhtsa_df, aes(y = sqrt(HIC15), x = DV_MPH)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Scatterplot of sqrt(HIC15) and DV_MPH", y = "sqrt(HIC15) (transformed)", x = "DV_MPH") + 
  # y axis to 65
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 40))


# scatterplot of HIC15_log and DV_MPH because above relationship was nonlinear
ggplot(madymo_nhtsa_df, aes(y = log(HIC15), x = DV_MPH)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Scatterplot of log(HIC15) and DV_MPH", y = "log(HIC15) (transformed)", x = "DV_MPH") + 
  # y axis to 65
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 12)) +
  # draw regression line
  geom_smooth(method = "lm")
```

```{r univariate logistic models, eval=FALSE}
# full univariate model
univariate_logistic_model_fun <- function(df) {
  glm(
    HIPR ~ (HIC15_log), 
    family = "binomial", 
    data = df)
}

# create dataframes for different subgroups
subgroups_df <- list(
  madymo_nhtsa_df,
  
  drivers_df = madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
    filter(SEATPOS == 11),

  passengers_df = madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
    filter(SEATPOS == 13),

  airbag_df = madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
    filter(BAGDEPLOY_BIN == 1),

  no_airbag_df = madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
    filter(BAGDEPLOY_BIN == 0),

  belt_df = madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
    filter(BELTUSE_BIN == 1),

  no_belt_df = madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
    filter(BELTUSE_BIN == 0),

  ten_twentyfive_df = madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
    filter(DV_MPH >= 10 & DV_MPH <= 25),

  twentyfive_forty_df = madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
    filter(DV_MPH >= 25 & DV_MPH <= 40),

  forty_fiftyfive_df = madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
    filter(DV_MPH >= 40 & DV_MPH <= 55),

  fiftyfive_df = madymo_nhtsa_df %>%
    filter(DV_MPH >= 55)
)



univariate_models <- list(
  logistic_model = 
    univariate_logistic_model_fun(madymo_nhtsa_df),
  
  # drivers only
  drivers_df_model = 
    univariate_logistic_model_fun(madymo_nhtsa_df[madymo_nhtsa_df$SEATPOS == 11,]),
  
  # passengers only
  passengers_df_model = 
    univariate_logistic_model_fun(madymo_nhtsa_df[madymo_nhtsa_df$SEATPOS == 13,]),
  
  # airbag only
  airbag_df_model = 
    univariate_logistic_model_fun(madymo_nhtsa_df[madymo_nhtsa_df$BAGDEPLOY_BIN == 1,]),
  
  # no airbag only
  no_airbag_df_model = 
    univariate_logistic_model_fun(madymo_nhtsa_df[madymo_nhtsa_df$BAGDEPLOY_BIN == 0,]),
  
  # belt only
  belt_df_model = 
    univariate_logistic_model_fun(madymo_nhtsa_df[madymo_nhtsa_df$BELTUSE_BIN == 1,]),
  
  # no belt only
  no_belt_df_model = 
    univariate_logistic_model_fun(madymo_nhtsa_df[madymo_nhtsa_df$BELTUSE_BIN == 0,]),
  
  # 10-25 kmh only DV_MPH
  ten_twentyfive_df_model = 
    univariate_logistic_model_fun(subgroups_df$ten_twentyfive_df),
  
  # 25-40 kmh only DV_MPH
  twentyfive_forty_df_model = 
    univariate_logistic_model_fun(subgroups_df$twentyfive_forty_df),
  
  # 40-55 kmh only DV_MPH
  forty_fiftyfive_df_model = 
    univariate_logistic_model_fun(subgroups_df$forty_fiftyfive_df),
  
  # 55+ kmh only DV_MPH
  fiftyfive_df_model = 
    univariate_logistic_model_fun(subgroups_df$fiftyfive_df)
)

summary(univariate_models$logistic_model)
summary(univariate_models$complex_logistic_model)
summary(univariate_models$drivers_df_model)
summary(univariate_models$passengers_df_model)
summary(univariate_models$airbag_df_model)
summary(univariate_models$no_airbag_df_model)
summary(univariate_models$belt_df_model)
summary(univariate_models$no_belt_df_model)
summary(univariate_models$ten_twentyfive_df_model)
summary(univariate_models$twentyfive_forty_df_model)
summary(univariate_models$forty_fiftyfive_df_model)
summary(univariate_models$fiftyfive_df_model)


# test complex logistic model
complex_logistic_model = glm(
  HIPR ~ HIC15_thousand + 
    BELTUSE_BIN + BAGDEPLOY_BIN + 
    SEATPOS + DV_MPH, 
  family = "binomial", 
  data = madymo_nhtsa_df)


OR_exp_β_fun <- function(model) {
    round(
      exp(
        model$coefficients[2]), 
      digits = 2)
}

ratio_exp_β_fun <- function(model1, model2) {
  round(
    exp(model1$coefficients[2])/
      exp(model2$coefficients[2]), 
    digits = 2)
}





# collect coefficients and p-values for all models, N count and %, avg HIC into a dataframe
model_summary_df$model <- c(
  "all",  "drivers", "passengers", "airbag", 
  "no_airbag", "belt", "no_belt", 
  "10-25 kmh", "25-40 kmh", "40-55 kmh", 
  "55+ kmh")

β <- c()
for (model in univariate_models) {
  β <-c(
    β,
    model$coefficients[2]
  )
}

p_value <- c()
for (model in univariate_models) {
  p_value <-c(
    p_value,
    coef(summary(model))[,4][2]
  )
}
  
OR_exp_β <- c()
for (model in univariate_models) {
  OR_exp_β <-c(
    OR_exp_β,
    round(
      exp(
        model$coefficients[2]), 
      digits = 2)
  )
}
  
N <- c()
for (model in subgroups_df) {
  N <- c(
    N,
    nrow(model)
  )
}

N_prct <- c()
for (model in subgroups_df) {
  N_prct <- c(
    N_prct,
    round(
      nrow(model)/
        nrow(madymo_nhtsa_df)*100, 
      digits = 2)
  )
}

avg_hic <- c()
for (model in subgroups_df) {
  avg_hic <- c(
    avg_hic,
    round(
      mean(model$HIC15, na.rm = TRUE),
      digits = 2)
  )
}

model_summary_df <- data.frame(
  β,
  p_value,
  OR_exp_β,
  N,
  N_prct,
  avg_hic
)

model_summary_df

# SD of HIC
sd(madymo_nhtsa_df$HIC15, na.rm = TRUE)

# histogram of HIC
ggplot(madymo_nhtsa_df, aes(x = HIC15)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 100) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of HIC15 Values", x = "HIC15", y = "Frequency")
# doesn't give much useful information except that it has a skewed distribution

# sensitivity and specificity of HIC15 vs HIPR
# calculate sensitivity and specificity
sensitivity <- sum(madymo_nhtsa_df$HIPR == 1 & madymo_nhtsa_df$HIC15 > 0) / sum(madymo_nhtsa_df$HIPR == 1)
library(pROC)
# ROC curve for continuous HIC variable vs HIPR
roc_obj <- roc(madymo_nhtsa_df$HIPR, madymo_nhtsa_df$HIC15)

```

```{r clean environment}
# clean unnecessary objects from enviroment
# clean out all items in environment except ones in kept_items
kept_items <- c("madymo_nhtsa_df", "MADYMO_df", "explore_df", "individual_count", "vehicle_count", "data_path_2000_2015", "univariate_models", "cols_final", "cols_all")
rm(list = setdiff(ls(), kept_items))
```


```{r plot coefficients and p-values}
# Check for convergence issues
# glance(univariate_models$belt_df_model) %>% 
#   select(null.deviance, df.residual, nobs)

# Check sample size and outcome distribution
# subgroups_df$belt_df %>% 
#   count(HIPR) %>% 
#   mutate(prop = n/sum(n))

# Check for complete separation
# table(subgroups_df$belt_df$HIPR, subgroups_df$belt_df$HIC15_thousand)


models <- list(
  "Full" = univariate_models$logistic_model,
  "Drivers" = univariate_models$drivers_df_model,
  "Passengers" = univariate_models$passengers_df_model,
  "Airbag" = univariate_models$airbag_df_model,
  "No Airbag" = univariate_models$no_airbag_df_model,
  "Belt" = univariate_models$belt_df_model,
  "No Belt" = univariate_models$no_belt_df_model,
  "10-25 kmh" = univariate_models$ten_twentyfive_df_model,
  "25-40 kmh" = univariate_models$twentyfive_forty_df_model,
  "40-55 kmh" = univariate_models$forty_fiftyfive_df_model,
  "55+ kmh" = univariate_models$fiftyfive_df_model
)



# ISSUE: BELT has no confidence levels in results_table

safe_tidy <- safely(~ tidy(.x, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE))

results_table <- map_dfr(models, ~ {
  result <- safe_tidy(.x)
  if (!is.null(result$error)) {
    tidy(.x, conf.int = FALSE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%  # Fallback without CIs
      mutate(conf.low = NA, conf.high = NA)
  } else {
    result$result
  }
}, .id = "Subgroup") %>% 
  filter(term == "HIC15_thousand") %>% 
  select(Subgroup, estimate, std.error, p.value, conf.low, conf.high)







# visualize odds ratios CIs per univariate model using 



ggplot(results_table, aes(x = estimate, y = Subgroup)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "Odds Ratio (95% CI)", y = "Subgroup", title = "Effect of HIC15_thousand on Injury Risk") +
  theme_minimal() +
  # only show -1 to 1
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.8, 1.4))
```

```{r print dataset to csv for external examination, eval=FALSE}
library(data.table)
# print madymo_nhtsa_df to csv
fwrite(madymo_nhtsa_df, file = file.path("data", "madymo_nhtsa_df.csv"))
```

```{r DAG causal model, eval=FALSE}
# Using dagitty
library(dagitty)
dag <- dagitty("dag {
  HIC15 -> HeadInjury
  SEATPOS -> HIC15
  BELTUSE_BIN -> HIC15
  BAGDEPLOY_BIN -> HIC15
  DV_MPH_BIN -> HIC15
  Age -> HeadInjury
  Sex -> HeadInjury
  BMI -> HeadInjury
}")
plot(dag)

# Using DiagrammeR
library(DiagrammeR)
grViz("
  digraph {
    HIC15 -> HeadInjury
    SEATPOS -> HIC15
    BELTUSE_BIN -> HIC15
    BAGDEPLOY_BIN -> HIC15
    DV_MPH_BIN -> HIC15
    Age -> HeadInjury
    Sex -> HeadInjury
    BMI -> HeadInjury
  }
")
```

```{r DAG validation model, eval=FALSE}
library(DiagrammeR)

grViz("
digraph DAG {
  # Nodes (entities)
  node [shape = rectangle, style = filled, fillcolor = 'lightblue']
  HIC15 [label = 'MADYMO HIC15'];
  HeadInjury [label = 'Real-World Head Injury'];
  
  node [fillcolor = 'lightyellow']
  seat_position; seatbelt_use; airbag_deployment; delta_V;
  
  node [fillcolor = 'lightpink']
  Age; Sex; BMI;
  
  # Edges (relationships)
  seat_position -> HIC15
  seatbelt_use -> HIC15
  airbag_deployment -> HIC15
  delta_V -> HIC15
  
  HIC15 -> HeadInjury
  
  seat_position -> HeadInjury
  seatbelt_use -> HeadInjury
  airbag_deployment -> HeadInjury
  delta_V -> HeadInjury
  
  Age -> HeadInjury
  Sex -> HeadInjury
  BMI -> HeadInjury
  
  # Hierarchical relationships
  {rank = same; seat_position; seatbelt_use; airbag_deployment; delta_V}
  {rank = same; Age; Sex; BMI}
}
")
```



```{}
# OLD SURVEY CODE
```
